title: Why is dummy plug refused during zeruel attack
display_title: Why is dummy plug refused during zeruel attack
wikitext:
[]: Why is dummy plug refused during zeruel attack?             Quote Post by Desty_Nova » Sun May 23, 2010 10:05 am OK a few things, well, a lot of things: Why did yui refuse the dummy sytem? Did she know Shinji would return? Did she even know he'd left? Could she even tell that an angel was attacking? What was her game plan here? If she does have the ability to refuse the dummy plug, why did she allow the eva-03 incident to occur the way it did? Why wasn't the umbilical cable used during the zeruel fight? If the external batteries are compact enough for a misson that involved crawling through tunnels why aren't they part of the evas standard loadout or built into the eva? Battery life is the evas achilles heel, a fix exists but they don't use it.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sun May 23, 2010 10:34 am Convenient plot device. The entry plug ejection is refused more times than not. Haven't you noticed that the nerv staff look shocked when it actually works?

[]: Re: Why is dummy plug refused during zeruel attack?             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun May 23, 2010 12:04 pm Desty_Nova wrote: Why did yui refuse the dummy sytem? Did she know Shinji would return? Did she even know he'd left? Could she even tell that an angel was attacking? What was her game plan here?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun May 23, 2010 12:04 pm Perhaps Yui was tricked the first time and couldn't pull out once she'd started, and wasn't willing to lose her control again.

[]:             Quote Post by Beibin » Sun May 23, 2010 8:25 pm In episode 19, they attempted to activate Eva-01 using the dummy plug system. In the previous episode it was used while the Eva was already in operation. Perhaps Yui thought Shinji was still piloting. So, could this mean that a pilot's thought patterns are indistinguishable, after the initial contact?

[]:             Quote Post by Itorbeh » Mon May 24, 2010 11:00 am The first time it was used there are two factors I believe which would be acceptable reasons for the system to work. 1) Yui was immediately able to tell the difference, the second time she was. 2) Yui allowed the system to take over in order for Unit 01 to defend itself/Shinji/defeat an angel (knowing Shinji was unable to at the time). Importantly, did Yui refuse the dummy system because of how Shinji felt about it or because it simply was not Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon May 24, 2010 2:55 pm Being inside Unit 01 is the safest place for Shinji to be during an Angel Attack. Therefore, Yui's going to refuse to fight if there's any possibility that Shinji could be harmed by the world outside the Entry Plug.

[]:             Quote Post by Agent_Koopa » Tue May 25, 2010 8:41 pm skikes wrote: Convenient plot device. The entry plug ejection is refused more times than not. Haven't you noticed that the nerv staff look shocked when it actually works?

[]:             Quote Post by IkariRei » Thu May 27, 2010 12:42 pm I've always though the Eva was just angry at Gendo because of the psychological pain the dummy plug activation during the fight with Eva 03 caused to Shinji.Sort of like a lover's quarrel.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 27, 2010 2:05 pm IkariRei wrote: I've always though the Eva was just angry at Gendo because of the psychological pain the dummy plug activation during the fight with Eva 03 caused to Shinji.Sort of like a lover's quarrel.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri May 28, 2010 6:26 am The rule seems to be that Yui can only take direct control of the Eva when it's unconscious (like when she protected Shinji from the debris in episode one). The first time the plug was used, the Eva was activated. But the second time, it wasn't, and she was able to refuse. That's the best excuse I can think of!

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Fri May 28, 2010 7:15 am Warren Peace wrote: The rule seems to be that Yui can only take direct control of the Eva when it's unconscious (like when she protected Shinji from the debris in episode one). The first time the plug was used, the Eva was activated. But the second time, it wasn't, and she was able to refuse. That's the best excuse I can think of!

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jun 07, 2010 10:11 am i tend to think she rejected the dummy plug because it's just not Shinji. i'm with aura on this one: the big difference is that in the unit-03 debacle, Shinji was IN the inserted entry plug; in episode 19, he wasn't. while Shinji is in the eva, he's in danger of getting killed if the angel wins-- yui wasn't going to let that happen so if Shinji wasn't going to fight, she had to do it for him, and in order for that to happen, she had to accept the dummy plug. in episode 19, Shinji wasn't around, so even if unit-01 did not fight, that didn't mean he would be imminently killed. gendo could find another way, or Shinji could come back (as it happened). however against bardiel, if she didn't accept the DP, Shinji died. quite simple.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jun 12, 2010 12:06 am Reichu wrote: Heh, that's kind of what I took from it, as well. It's like Gendo headed to the Cage right after that so he could give Yui a stern talking to.

[]:             Quote Post by Desty_Nova » Sat Jun 12, 2010 12:27 pm IIRC she refuses Rei as well. Maybe she cannot distinguish between Rei and the dummy system?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jun 12, 2010 3:02 pm She does reject Rei, but she's synced with her before, so I'm sure she can tell the difference. The simple fact of the matter is that she wants Shinji.
